zh.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady於1095年出生在英國。她和John de Harley Sir至少有1個兒子。她去世於大約1138年在她的家鄉享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady于1095年出生在英国。她和John de Harley Sir至少有1个儿子。她去世于大约1138年在她的家乡享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leighton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Leighton or Layton. Most, as for example those in Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Lancashire, and Shropshire, are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘settlement’, but Layton in Durham derives from Old English ge-lād ‘road’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English pronunciation of Leighton is usually /lɛ:tən/ or /leitən/ (also spelled Layton and Leaton ), but it can also be heard as /laitən/ or as /li:tən/ (also spelled Leaton ), and occasionally as /lɛtən/ (spelled Letton ).

variants.name: Layton
meanings: English: variant of   Leighton  .

variants.name: Slayton
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Leighton (compare Layton ) with the addition of initial S-. Alternatively, perhaps a variant of Sladen or of Sleightholme, principally a habitational name from Sleightholme Dale in Kirkbymoorside, but perhaps occasionally from Sleightholme in Bowes or the lost Sleightholme in Lythe. The placenames derive from Old Norse sléttr ‘smooth, level’ + holmr ‘small island, water meadow’. This surname is now very rare in Britain. Compare Slaydon .

variants.name: Leaton
meanings: English: variant of   Leighton  .

variants.name: Laughton
meanings: English:

habitational name from Laughton en le Morthen in Yorkshire, Laughton in Leicestershire or any of the two places called Laughton in Lincolnshire (one near Gainsborough and another near Folkingham). The places are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘enclosure’, meaning ‘village or farm where leeks are grown’. It may sometimes have alternated with Leighton , which is mostly from placenames with the same etymology.
variant of   Lawton  .

variants.name: Letton
meanings: English: habitational name, most probably from Letton in Norfolk or Latton in Essex. It could also be a variant of Leighton or Laughton . Most of the places so named have the same etymology as Letton and Latton, i.e. ‘farm where leeks are grown’ (Old English lēac-tūn). Another possibility is that it is a variant of Latten .

nameMeanings.name: "de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harley"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places called Harley (Shropshire, Yorkshire). The Shropshire placename derives from Old English hær ‘rock, heap of stones’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. The Yorkshire placename derives from Old English hara ‘hare’ or hær + hlāw ‘mound, hill’, later replaced by lēah.

Irish (Donegal): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarghaile ‘descendant of Earghal’, a variant of the personal name Fearghal without the initial F-. This name has also become Herley and Hurley . Compare Arrell and Harrell .
Scottish (Fife): habitational name from any of several places called Harlaw in Midlothian, Peeblesshire, and Berwickshire, named in Older Scots with hare ‘gray’ + law ‘hill’.

variants.name: Herley
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Art, Cormac.
Irish: variant of   Hurley  , probably via   Herlihy  .
English: variant of   Harley   or   Hurley  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady was born in 1095, in England. She had at least 1 son with John de Harley Sir. She died about 1138, in her hometown, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady est née en 1095, à Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec John de Harley Sir. Elle est décédée environ 1138, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady wurde 1095 in England geboren. Sie hatte mit John de Harley Sir mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1138 im Alter von 44 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady è nata il nel 1095 nel England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con John de Harley Sir. Morì circa 1138, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 44 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady(은)는 1095년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John de Harley Sir(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1138년 그녀의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady nasceu em 1095, em Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com John de Harley Sir. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1138, em sua cidade natal, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady родилась в 1095 г. в местности Англия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от John de Harley Sir. Она умерла около 1138 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Alice de Leighton "de Harley" Lady nació en 1095, en Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con John de Harley Sir. Murió aproximadamente 1138, en su pueblo, a la edad de 44 años.
